evals.prompt: Make the software archeology of this repository. Generate the executions graph.
evals.expected_output: Full archaeology workflow: (1) Appends a new entry to docs/findings/FINDINGS.md before writing to knowledge/; (2) Runs or invokes retro-engineer for automated analysis; (3) Produces an executions graph (Graphviz dot or Mermaid) showing entry points → core modules → external APIs; (4) Creates a call tree; (5) Generates an external API inventory; (6) Updates context/run_context.md with confirmed facts; (7) Identifies hardware signals and dispatches to hardware-analyst if present.

assertions.text: docs/findings/FINDINGS.md is updated with a new F-XXX entry BEFORE writing to knowledge/
An executions graph is produced (dot, mermaid, or ASCII)
An external API inventory is included (every system call with file and line)
context/run_context.md is updated with confirmed technology stack facts

evals.prompt: Backtrack this codebase to understand why `process_payment()` calls `validate_card()` before `charge()`. What's the execution path?
evals.expected_output: Targeted backtracking: (1) Locate process_payment() entry point; (2) Trace the call chain: process_payment → validate_card → (card validation logic) → charge → (payment gateway API); (3) Identify any guards or preconditions; (4) Document the decision (validate before charge = fail-fast pattern, security mandate); (5) Add finding to FINDINGS.md. The output shows the full call tree from process_payment down to external APIs.

assertions.text: The full call chain from process_payment to the payment gateway is documented
The reason for validate-before-charge ordering is identified (fail-fast or security mandate)
